description: There is an m x n rectangular island that borders both the Pacific Ocean and Atlantic Ocean. Given the heights of the island, return a list of grid coordinates where rain water can flow to both the Pacific and Atlantic oceans.

name: Sentence Screen Fitting
description: Given a rows x cols screen and a sentence represented as a list of strings, return the number of times the given sentence can be fitted on the screen.

name: Battleships in a Board
description: Given an m x n matrix board where each cell is a battleship 'X' or empty '.', return the number of the battleships on board.

name: Strong Password Checker
description: A password is considered strong if it has at least 6 characters, at most 20 characters, at least one lowercase letter, one uppercase letter, and one digit, and does not have three repeating characters in a row. Return the minimum number of steps to make the password strong.

name: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
description: Given an integer array nums, return the maximum result of nums[i] XOR nums[j], where 0 <= i <= j < n.

name: Valid Word Square
description: Given an array of strings words, return true if it forms a valid word square. A sequence of strings forms a valid word square if the kth row and column read the same string.

name: Reconstruct Original Digits from English
description: Given a string s containing an out-of-order English representation of digits 0-9, return the digits in ascending order.

name: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
description: You are given a string s and an integer k. You can choose any character of the string and change it to any other uppercase English character at most k times. Return the length of the longest substring containing the same letter after performing the operations.

name: Word Squares
description: Given an array of unique strings words, return all the word squares you can build from words. A sequence of strings forms a valid word square if the kth row and column read the same string.

name: Convert Binary Search Tree to Sorted Doubly Linked List
description: Convert a Binary Search Tree to a sorted Circular Doubly-Linked List in place.

name: Construct Quad Tree
description: Given an n x n matrix grid of 0's and 1's only, we want to represent the grid with a Quad-Tree. Return the root of the Quad-Tree representing the grid.

name: Serialize and Deserialize N-ary Tree
description: Serialization is the process of converting a data structure into a sequence of bits. Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize an N-ary tree.

name: N-ary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Given an n-ary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values.

name: Flatten a Multilevel Doubly Linked List
description: You are given a doubly linked list, which contains nodes that have a next pointer, a previous pointer, and an additional child pointer. Flatten the list so that all nodes appear in a single-level doubly linked list.

name: Encode N-ary Tree to Binary Tree
description: Design an algorithm to encode an N-ary tree into a binary tree and decode the binary tree to get the original N-ary tree.

name: All O'one Data Structure
description: Design a data structure to store the strings' count with the ability to return the strings with minimum and maximum counts. Implement AllOne with inc, dec, getMaxKey, and getMinKey methods.

name: Minimum Genetic Mutation
description: A gene string can be represented by an 8-character long string. Given the two gene strings startGene and endGene and a gene bank bank, return the minimum number of mutations needed to mutate from startGene to endGene.

name: Number of Segments in a String
description: Given a string s, return the number of segments in the string. A segment is defined to be a contiguous sequence of non-space characters.

name: Non-overlapping Intervals
description: Given an array of intervals intervals where intervals[i] = [starti, endi], return the minimum number of intervals you need to remove to make the rest of the intervals non-overlapping.

name: Find Right Interval
description: You are given an array of intervals, where intervals[i] = [starti, endi] and each starti is unique. For each interval i, check if there exists an interval j whose start point is greater than or equal to the end point of the interval i.

name: Path Sum III
description: Given the root of a binary tree and an integer targetSum, return the number of paths where the sum of the values along the path equals targetSum. The path does not need to start or end at the root or a leaf.

name: Find All Anagrams in a String
description: Given two strings s and p, return an array of all the start indices of p's anagrams in s. You may return the answer in any order.

name: Ternary Expression Parser
description: Given a string expression representing arbitrarily nested ternary expressions, evaluate the expression and return its result as a string.

name: K-th Smallest in Lexicographical Order
description: Given two integers n and k, return the kth lexicographically smallest integer in the range [1, n].

name: Arranging Coins
description: You have n coins and you want to build a staircase with these coins. The staircase consists of k rows where the ith row has exactly i coins. Return the number of complete rows of the staircase you will build.

name: Find All Duplicates in an Array
description: Given an integer array nums of length n where all the integers of nums are in the range [1, n] and each integer appears at most twice, return an array of all the integers that appear twice.

name: String Compression
description: Given an array of characters chars, compress it using the following algorithm: For each group of consecutive repeating characters, replace them with the character followed by its count.

name: Sequence Reconstruction
description: You are given an integer array nums of length n where nums is a permutation of [1, n]. You are also given a 2D integer array sequences. Check if nums is the only shortest supersequence.

name: Add Two Numbers II
description: You are given two non-empty linked lists representing two non-negative integers. The most significant digit comes first. Add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list.

name: Arithmetic Slices II - Subsequence
description: Given an integer array nums, return the number of all the arithmetic subsequences of nums. A sequence of at least three numbers is called arithmetic if its differences are the same.

name: Number of Boomerangs
description: You are given n points in the plane that are all distinct, where points[i] = [xi, yi]. A boomerang is a tuple of points (i, j, k) such that the distance between i and j equals the distance between i and k. Return the number of boomerangs.

name: Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array
description: Given an array nums of n integers where nums[i] is in the range [1, n], return an array of all the integers in the range [1, n] that do not appear in nums.

name: Serialize and Deserialize BST
description: Serialization is converting a data structure into a sequence of bits. Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize a binary search tree.

name: Delete Node in a BST
description: Given a root node reference of a BST and a key, delete the node with the given key in the BST. Return the root node reference (possibly updated) of the BST.

name: Sort Characters By Frequency
description: Given a string s, sort it in decreasing order based on the frequency of the characters. The frequency of a character is the number of times it appears in the string.

name: Minimum Number of Arrows to Burst Balloons
description: There are some spherical balloons taped onto a flat wall that represents the XY-plane. Return the minimum number of arrows that must be shot to burst all balloons.

name: Minimum Moves to Equal Array Elements
description: Given an integer array nums of size n, return the minimum number of moves required to make all array elements equal. In one move, you can increment n - 1 elements of the array by 1.

name: 4Sum II
description: Given four integer arrays nums1, nums2, nums3, and nums4 all of length n, return the number of tuples (i, j, k, l) such that nums1[i] + nums2[j] + nums3[k] + nums4[l] == 0.

name: Assign Cookies
description: Assume you are an awesome parent and want to give your children some cookies. Each child i has a greed factor g[i]. Each cookie j has a size s[j]. Return the maximum number of your content children.

name: 132 Pattern
description: Given an array of n integers nums, a 132 pattern is a subsequence of three integers nums[i], nums[j] and nums[k] such that i < j < k and nums[i] < nums[k] < nums[j]. Return true if there is a 132 pattern in nums.

name: Circular Array Loop
description: You are playing a game involving a circular array of non-zero integers nums. Return true if there is a cycle in nums, or false otherwise.

name: Poor Pigs
description: There are buckets buckets of liquid, where exactly one of them is poisonous. Given minutesToTest minutes to test and minutesToDie minutes for a pig to die, return the minimum number of pigs needed to figure out which bucket is poisonous.

name: Repeated Substring Pattern
description: Given a string s, check if it can be constructed by taking a substring of it and appending multiple copies of the substring together.

name: LFU Cache
description: Design and implement a data structure for a Least Frequently Used (LFU) cache. Implement the LFUCache class with get and put methods, both in O(1) time.

name: Hamming Distance
description: The Hamming distance between two integers is the number of positions at which the corresponding bits are different. Given two integers x and y, return the Hamming distance between them.

name: Minimum Moves to Equal Array Elements II
description: Given an integer array nums of size n, return the minimum number of moves required to make all array elements equal. In one move, you can increment or decrement an element of the array by 1.

name: Island Perimeter
description: You are given row x col grid representing a map where grid[i][j] = 1 represents land and grid[i][j] = 0 represents water. Return the perimeter of the island.

name: Can I Win
description: In the 100 game two players take turns choosing from integers 1 to 10, and the first player to reach a total >= desiredTotal wins. Given maxChoosableInteger and desiredTotal, determine if the first player can guarantee a win.

name: Optimal Account Balancing
description: You are given an array of transactions where transactions[i] = [fromi, toi, amounti]. Return the minimum number of transactions required to settle the debt.

name: Count The Repetitions
description: We define str = [s, n] as the string str which consists of the string s concatenated n times. Given four integers n1, n2, and strings s1, s2, find the maximum integer m such that str = [s2, m] can be obtained from str = [s1, n1].

name: Unique Substrings in Wraparound String
description: We define the string base to be the infinite wraparound string of 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'. Given a string s, return the number of unique non-empty substrings of s that are present in base.

name: Validate IP Address
description: Given a string queryIP, return 'IPv4' if IP is a valid IPv4 address, 'IPv6' if IP is a valid IPv6 address, or 'Neither' if IP is not a correct IP of any type.

name: Convex Polygon
description: You are given an array of points on the X-Y plane points where points[i] = [xi, yi]. Return true if these points form a convex polygon.

name: Implement Rand10() Using Rand7()
description: Given the API rand7() that generates a uniform random integer in the range [1, 7], write a function rand10() that generates a uniform random integer in the range [1, 10].

name: Encode String with Shortest Length
description: Given a string s, encode the string such that its encoded length is the shortest. The encoding rule is: k[encoded_string], where the encoded_string inside the square brackets is being repeated exactly k times.

name: Concatenated Words
description: Given an array of strings words (without duplicates), return all the concatenated words in the given list of words. A concatenated word is defined as a string that is comprised entirely of at least two shorter words in the given array.

name: Matchsticks to Square
description: You are given an integer array matchsticks where matchsticks[i] is the length of the ith matchstick. Use all the matchsticks to make one square. Return true if you can make this square and false otherwise.

name: Ones and Zeroes
description: You are given an array of binary strings strs and two integers m and n. Return the size of the largest subset of strs such that there are at most m 0's and n 1's in the subset.

name: Heaters
description: Winter is coming! Given positions of houses and heaters on a horizontal line, return the minimum radius standard of heaters so that those heaters could cover all houses.

name: Number Complement
description: Given a positive integer num, return its complement. The complement of an integer is obtained by flipping all bits of its binary representation.

name: Total Hamming Distance
description: The Hamming distance between two integers is the number of positions at which the corresponding bits are different. Given an integer array nums, return the sum of Hamming distances between all the pairs of the integers in nums.

name: Generate Random Point in a Circle
description: Given the radius and the position of the center of a circle, implement the function randPoint which generates a uniform random point inside the circle.

name: Largest Palindrome Product
description: Given an integer n, return the largest palindromic integer that can be represented as the product of two n-digits integers. Since the answer can be very large, return it modulo 1337.

name: Sliding Window Median
description: The median is the middle value in an ordered integer list. Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, there is a sliding window of size k moving from the very left to the very right. Return the median array for each window.

name: Magical String
description: A magical string s consists of only '1' and '2' and obeys the rule: the string is self-describing, meaning the number of 1s and 2s in each group corresponds to the values in the sequence itself. Return the number of 1's in the first n numbers in the magical string s.

name: License Key Formatting
description: You are given a license key represented as a string s that consists of only alphanumeric characters and dashes. Given an integer k, reformat the string by grouping characters into blocks of k.

name: Smallest Good Base
description: Given an integer n represented as a string, return the smallest good base of n. An integer base b is a good base if all digits of n in base b are 1's.

name: Find Permutation
description: A permutation perm of n integers of all the integers in the range [1, n] can be represented as a string s of length n - 1. Return any permutation perm that satisfies the given hint string s.

name: Max Consecutive Ones
description: Given a binary array nums, return the maximum number of consecutive 1's in the array.

name: Predict the Winner
description: You are given an integer array nums. Two players are playing a game with this array: player 1 and player 2. Return true if Player 1 can win the game, assuming both players play optimally.

name: Max Consecutive Ones II
description: Given a binary array nums, return the maximum number of consecutive 1's in the array if you can flip at most one 0.

name: Zuma Game
description: You have a board of colored balls. On each turn, you can insert a ball of any color anywhere. Return the minimum number of balls you have to insert to clear all the balls from the board, or -1 if you cannot clear all the balls.

name: Robot Room Cleaner
description: You are controlling a robot that is located somewhere in a room. The room is modeled as an m x n binary grid where 0 indicates a wall and 1 indicates an empty slot. Design an algorithm to clean the entire room using the robot.

name: The Maze
description: There is a ball in a maze with empty spaces (represented as 0) and walls (represented as 1). Return true if the ball can stop at the destination, otherwise return false.

name: Non-decreasing Subsequences
description: Given an integer array nums, return all the different possible non-decreasing subsequences of the given array with at least two elements.

name: Construct the Rectangle
description: Given a specific rectangular web page's area, your job is to design a rectangular web page, whose length L and width W satisfy the following requirements: L >= W, L * W = area, L - W is minimized.

name: Reverse Pairs
description: Given an integer array nums, return the number of reverse pairs in the array. A reverse pair is a pair (i, j) where i < j and nums[i] > 2 * nums[j].

name: Target Sum
description: You are given an integer array nums and an integer target. You want to build an expression out of nums by adding one of the symbols '+' and '-' before each integer. Return the number of different expressions that you can build which evaluate to target.

name: Teemo Attacking
description: Our hero Teemo is attacking an enemy Ashe with poison attacks. Given timeSeries array and an integer duration, return the number of seconds that Ashe is poisoned.

name: Next Greater Element I
description: The next greater element of some element x in an array is the first greater element to the right of x in the same array. Given two distinct 0-indexed integer arrays nums1 and nums2, return the next greater element for each element in nums1.

name: Random Point in Non-overlapping Rectangles
description: You are given an array of non-overlapping axis-aligned rectangles rects. Implement the Solution class that picks a random integer point inside a random rectangle.

name: Diagonal Traverse
description: Given an m x n matrix mat, return an array of all the elements of the array in a diagonal order.

name: The Maze III
description: There is a ball in a maze with empty spaces (represented as 0) and walls (represented as 1). Return the shortest path instruction for the ball to stop at the destination, or 'impossible' if it's not possible.

name: Keyboard Row
description: Given an array of strings words, return the words that can be typed using letters of the alphabet on only one row of American keyboard.
